pages.raw_text:
That the lumber industry is thriv-
ing in this city can readily be seen
by looking at the boom of the Saw
Mill Phoenix below Front avenue.
There logs are damned together as
close as they can be packed, extend
ing In line for nearly three blocks.


Over 100000 feet of Logs are in
the boom. The saw mill officials say
they are taxed to the utmost to keep
up with their orders.


The logs come from Springdale on
the Spokane Falls 4 Northern and
are slid into the water from the cars
up by the Division street bridge. They
then commence their journey down
the boom, which only ends when the
big grapples catch them and haul
them up to the mill.


An official of the mill said yester-
day they We're saving from 10000
to $4,000 feet of lumber a day. This
will be increased when the weather
moderates and the logs thaw out.
